title: 9 Best Natural Remedies for Cold Sores and Colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for colds because it helps to reduce inflammation and fight off viruses by boosting the immune system and producing fewer symptoms such as sore throats and congestion.
Catechins are good for colds because they help boost the immune system by fighting off viruses and reducing inflammation in the body, which makes it easier for the body to recover from a cold.
Eucalyptol is good for colds because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Cineole is good for colds because it helps to break down mucus in the nose and throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion and coughs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams are a popular remedy for colds. This is because they contain a strong-smelling oil called eucalyptol. When applied topically to the chest and nose, eucalyptol helps to ease congestion by thinning and loosening mucus. This makes it easier to breathe and expel mucus from the body. The menthol in the cream also helps to open up airways, allowing more air to reach the lungs. As a result, people who use eucalyptus globulus creams often report feeling more comfortable and able to breathe easier. The cream is also easy to apply and can be used as often as needed. Some people find it helpful to apply it before bed, so that they can breathe more easily while they sleep.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal creams that help with colds, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Echinacea angustifolia roots, coconut oil, beeswax, and essential oils like lavender or peppermint. Begin by steeping the dried Echinacea roots in a carrier oil, such as coconut oil, for several weeks to extract their medicinal properties. Strain the mixture and discard the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Add the infused oil and stir until the mixture is smooth and well combined. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Pour the mixture into a tin or jar and let it cool and harden completely. Once set, you can add a few drops of your chosen essential oil and mix well. Your Echinacea angustifolia herbal cream is now ready to use - simply apply it to your skin as needed to help soothe and calm cold symptoms, such as sore throats and congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Flavonoids are good for colds because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off viral infections in the body.
Quercetin is good for colds because it helps reduce inflammation and fight off the infection by boosting the body's immune system, making it easier to recover from the illness.
Polyphenols are good for colds because they help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making the body less likely to get sick and recover faster when infected.
Tannins are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nose and throat, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea cream is a natural remedy that can help your body fight off colds. It is made from the flowers of the Echinacea plant, which has been used for centuries to boost the immune system. When you put the cream on your skin, it helps to soothe and calm your body. The cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and pain in your throat and sinuses. It also has antiviral properties, which can help to stop the cold virus from spreading. When you apply the cream, the active ingredients are absorbed into your skin and then travel to your bloodstream, where they can help to fight off the cold virus. This can help to reduce the severity and duration of your cold symptoms, such as congestion, cough, and sore throat. By using Echinacea purpurea cream, you may be able to recover from your cold faster and feel better sooner. The cream is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for most people to use, including children and pregnant women.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal creams to help with colds, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Echinacea angustifolia roots, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, shea butter or cocoa butter, and essential oils like peppermint or eucalyptus. First, prepare the Echinacea root by finely grinding it in a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine the ground root with the carrier oil in a saucepan and heat it gently over low heat, allowing the mixture to simmer for 30 minutes to an hour. This process is called infusion, and it helps to release the active compounds from the Echinacea root. After the infusion is complete, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids. Then, in a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax and shea butter or cocoa butter over low heat, stirring occasionally. Once both the infused liquid and the melted wax mixture are ready, combine them in a mixing bowl and whip the mixture until it thickens and cools. Finally, add a few drops of the essential oil and pour the herbal cream into a clean container, allowing it to cool and solidify completely before use.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for colds because it has antimicrobial properties that help fight off the viruses and bacteria that cause infections and reduce inflammation in the body.
Carvacrol helps fight colds because it has strong antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and soothe the throat.
Rosmarinic acid is good for colds because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and fight off the infection that causes a sore throat and congestion.
Tannins are good for colds because they help reduce inflammation and fight off infections by acting as a natural antioxidant that boosts the immune system and prevents the spread of viruses.
Caryophyllene is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the throat and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris cream, also known as thyme cream, is a natural remedy that can help with colds. This cream contains thymol, a compound found in thyme plants. Thymol has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help fight off the infection causing your cold. When applied topically, thymol can soothe a sore throat and reduce congestion. It can also help to loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up. Thyme cream can be especially helpful when used in combination with other cold-fighting remedies, such as rest and hydration. Additionally, thymol's antibacterial properties can help prevent the spread of infection, reducing the risk of complications.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal cream that helps with colds, start by drying and preparing the Echinacea roots. Harvest the roots in the fall or early spring, then wash and dry them thoroughly. Next, chop the dried roots into small pieces and steep them in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, for several weeks. This process is called maceration, and it helps to extract the beneficial properties from the Echinacea roots into the oil. Once the maceration process is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids. Then, mix the infused oil with a cream base, such as beeswax and coconut oil, in a double boiler. Heat the mixture until the beeswax is fully melted, then remove it from the heat and let it cool. As it cools, the mixture will thicken into a cream. Finally, transfer the cream to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to prevent spoilage. This Echinacea angustifolia herbal cream can be applied topically to help soothe and calm cold symptoms, such as sore throats and congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Shogaols help prevent and reduce the severity of colds by fighting off the infection, reducing inflammation, and boosting the immune system.
Volatile oils are good for colds because they have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion, soothe a sore throat, and fight off the infection that causes colds.
Polyphenols are good for colds because they have strong antioxidant properties that help boost the immune system, reducing inflammation and fighting off the viruses that cause colds.

Zingiberene is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and feel better when you have a cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, is a popular remedy for colds. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and ease a sore throat. When applied topically as a cream, ginger can help loosen mucus and make it easier to breathe. Ginger also contains compounds that help reduce pain and discomfort, making it a great relief for headaches and body aches that often come with a cold. Additionally, ginger has antimicrobial properties that help fight off the underlying infection that causes a cold. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the infection, ginger cream can help alleviate cold symptoms and promote faster recovery.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal cream to help with colds, you'll need dried Echinacea roots, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other natural ingredients. Start by infusing the dried Echinacea roots in a carrier oil for a few weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the roots release their goodness into the oil. Strain the mixture and discard the roots. Next, mix the infused oil with beeswax in a double boiler, heating the mixture until the beeswax has melted. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Now, add a few drops of vitamin E oil and a teaspoon of honey, stirring well to combine. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely, allowing the cream to set. Once set, you can apply the Echinacea angustifolia cream to your skin to help boost your immune system and alleviate cold symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosalinic acid is good for colds because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making breathing easier and helping to relieve cold symptoms.
Carnosic acid is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling, ease congestion, and protect the body from damage caused by cold viruses.

Polyphenols are good for colds because they have strong antioxidant properties that help boost the immune system, fight off infections, and reduce inflammation in the body, making it easier to recover from cold and flu symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with health problems. Its cream form is a great way to use its benefits to fight colds. The cream works by helping to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a big part of what makes a cold so uncomfortable. When we're sick with a cold, our airways can get swollen and congested, making it hard to breathe. Rosemary cream can help to ease this swelling and make it easier to breathe. It also has antimicrobial properties, which means it can help to kill the germs that are causing the cold in the first place. This can help to shorten the length of time we're sick and make us feel better faster. Additionally, rosemary cream has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe a sore throat and make it feel better. It can also help to ease a cough and make it easier to cough up mucus, which is another part of the cold that can be uncomfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal cream to help with colds, first, you need to dry and prepare the Echinacea roots. You can buy dried Echinacea angustifolia roots from a health food store or online. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea roots with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid and discard the solids. Allow the liquid to cool down, then mix it with 1 cup of petroleum jelly or coconut oil in a separate container. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 10-15 seconds, or until the mixture is smooth and well combined. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool down completely. Once cooled, your Echinacea herbal cream is ready to use. You can apply it to your skin to help soothe and calm cold symptoms, but remember to do a patch test before using it extensively.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Triterpenoids are good for colds because they help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, which can help fight off the virus and relieve cold symptoms.
Polyphenols are good for colds because they help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections in the body, making it easier for people to recover from a cold.
Saponins are good for colds because they help boost the immune system, which fights off the virus that causes colds and helps the body recover faster.
Flavonoids are good for colds because they help boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and fight off the viruses that cause colds, making it easier for the body to recover and feel better.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams are a popular remedy for colds because they contain a powerful ingredient called glycyrrhizin. This ingredient has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nose and sinuses. When you have a cold, your body produces more mucus, which can be difficult to breathe through. Glycyrrhizin in the cream helps break down this mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion. The cream also helps soothe a sore throat and calm a cough. Glycyrrhizin has antiviral properties that help fight off the virus that causes the cold. This means that the cream can help reduce the severity and duration of the cold symptoms. Additionally, the cream is often combined with other ingredients like menthol and eucalyptus, which have decongestant properties that help clear out your airways. When used regularly, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide quick relief from cold symptoms, making it easier to recover from a cold.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal creams that help with colds, you will need dried roots of the Echinacea angustifolia plant and a few basic supplies. First, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea roots with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container. Let it steep for 10 to 15 minutes, then strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Allow the liquid to cool down completely. Next, mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until the beeswax dissolves. Now, slowly add the cooled Echinacea liquid to the beeswax mixture and stir well. Continue to heat the mixture gently until it thickens and reaches a consistency similar to a lotion. Remove the saucepan from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken further. Once it has cooled and thickened, transfer the Echinacea cream to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to keep it fresh. When needed, simply apply a small amount to your skin to help soothe and calm cold symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for colds because it helps to open up airways and fight off viruses, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Quercetin is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the infection causing the cold.
Terpinen-4-ol is good for colds because it has strong antiviral properties that help to fight off the viruses that cause colds and reduce inflammation in the body.
Catechins are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infections in the body, making it easier to recover from a cold.
Tannins may be good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream, also known as tea tree oil cream, has been used to help relieve cold symptoms for a long time. It contains a powerful antiviral and antibacterial agent called eucalyptol, which helps to fight off the viruses and bacteria that cause colds. When applied to the skin, this cream can help to reduce congestion and ease a sore throat. Its cooling and soothing properties can also help to calm a cough and make it easier to breathe. Furthermore, tea tree oil cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and redness in the nose and sinuses. This can make it easier to breathe and can also help to reduce sinus pressure and headaches associated with colds.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal creams that help with colds, you'll need dried Echinacea roots, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other simple ingredients. First, you'll need to make an infusion by steeping the dried Echinacea roots in the carrier oil for several hours or overnight. Then, strain the oil and discard the roots. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in the microwave in short intervals, being careful not to burn it. Once the beeswax is melted, add the infused oil and any other desired ingredients like vitamin E oil or essential oils like eucalyptus or peppermint. Stir well to combine and pour the mixture into small containers. Let the mixture cool and harden before using. When you need to use the cream, just gently massage it onto your skin, paying special attention to areas like your chest and nose that are prone to cold symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for colds because it has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, ease congestion, and boost the immune system.
Tannins are good for colds because they help reduce inflammation and fight off viruses that cause respiratory infections, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Polyphenols are good for colds because they have strong antioxidant properties that can help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation in the body.

Isorhapontigenin is good for colds because it helps to protect the body from infection and inflammation, and also has antioxidant properties that can help to reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms.
Anthocyanins are good for colds because they have strong antioxidant properties that help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making it easier for the body to fight off cold viruses.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra cream, also known as elderberry cream, is used to help relieve cold symptoms. Elderberry is rich in antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation in the body. This can make it easier to breathe and reduce congestion. The cream can be applied directly to the skin, typically on the chest or back, to ease coughs and sore throats. The active ingredients in elderberry cream may also help to break down mucus, making it easier to expel. By reducing inflammation and breaking down mucus, elderberry cream can help alleviate cold symptoms and promote a faster recovery. The cream is often used in combination with other cold remedies to help manage symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal cream that helps with colds, first dry the roots and flowers of the Echinacea angustifolia plant thoroughly. Then, combine 1 part of dried Echinacea roots and flowers with 4 parts of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar. Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days, to allow the Echinacea to infuse into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and add 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax to the infused oil. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals until the beeswax is melted and fully incorporated. Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Pour the cream into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely before use. This Echinacea angustifolia herbal cream can be applied topically to the skin to help alleviate cold symptoms and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal creams for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cold.
Alkylamides are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the body.

Alkaloids are good for colds because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nose and throat, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia cream is often used to help people feel better when they have a cold. It's made from the root of the Echinacea plant, which is native to North America. This plant has been used for centuries by Native Americans to boost their immune system and fight off illness. The cream is applied directly to the skin, usually on the chest, throat, or nose. It's thought to work by reducing inflammation and fighting off the germs that cause colds. Some people believe that it helps to ease congestion and coughs, while others think it helps to reduce the severity of cold symptoms. When applied to the skin, the cream may help to ease a sore throat and ease body aches. Some people also use it to help their body recover from the common cold.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal cream for colds, you will need dried Echinacea roots, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, a double boiler, and a glass jar for storage. First, make a herbal infusion by combining one tablespoon of dried Echinacea roots with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid into a separate container and discard the solids. Next, in a double boiler, melt one tablespoon of beeswax and one cup of the carrier oil. Once the mixture has melted, slowly add the Echinacea infusion and stir until it is well combined. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring it into a glass jar. Allow the cream to cool and harden completely before use. Once hardened, apply the Echinacea cream to the skin as needed to help soothe and calm cold symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea angustifolia herbal creams for colds

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great help when you have a cold. Two important herbs in these creams are Eucalyptus globulus and Echinacea purpurea. Eucalyptus globulus helps to relieve congestion by opening up airways, making it easier to breathe. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the nose and sinuses. Echinacea purpurea is an immune system booster, helping your body fight off the cold virus. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the severity of cold symptoms.  Using herbal creams with these herbs can help you feel better faster. By relieving congestion and reducing inflammation, you can breathe more easily and feel more comfortable. This can improve your quality of life during a cold, allowing you to rest and recover. By boosting your immune system, you can also reduce the risk of getting more severe illnesses.
supplementary_best: To remedy colds, the best combination of herbal creams includes Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, and Thymus vulgaris. Eucalyptus globulus helps to clear congestion by releasing its decongestant properties, which are effective in breaking down mucus. Echinacea purpurea is an immune system booster, and its addition helps to fight off the underlying infection causing the cold. Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, has antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacterial and viral infections, reducing the severity and duration of the cold. This combination creates a synergistic effect, where the properties of each herb work together to provide a more effective remedy for colds. The decongestant properties of Eucalyptus globulus help to clear the airways, allowing the thymol in thyme (Thymus vulgaris) to work more effectively, and the immune-boosting properties of Echinacea purpurea help to support the body in fighting off the infection.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a cold, there are some herbal creams to avoid. These creams contain ingredients that can make your symptoms worse or interact with medications. For example, creams containing 'Hydrastis canadensis' (goldenseal) should be avoided because they can cause stomach problems and interact with blood thinners. Similarly, 'Arctium lappa' (burdock root) can cause allergic reactions and interact with diabetes medications. 'Angelica sinensis' (dong quai) can slow down blood clotting and interact with medications, such as aspirin, warfarin, and heparin. 'Atractylis gummifera' (devil's claw) can interact with blood thinners and diabetes medications, and 'Ginkgo biloba' can cause bleeding and interact with medications like aspirin and warfarin. These herbal creams should be used with caution or avoided if you have a cold.
supplementary_other_remedies: When we have a cold, our bodies need extra help to fight off the infection. Besides using herbal creams, there are other natural remedies that can provide relief and support. Epsom salt baths can be very helpful because they help to reduce inflammation and open up the airways, making it easier to breathe. The magnesium in the Epsom salt also helps to relax the body and reduce muscle tension, which can be a big relief when we're feeling unwell. Drinking plenty of warm water with lemon and honey can also be beneficial, as the acidity in the lemon helps to break down mucus and the honey soothes the throat. Additionally, some people find that inhaling the steam from a bowl of hot water with a few drops of eucalyptus oil can help to loosen up congestion and make it easier to breathe. This is because the eucalyptus oil has natural decongestant properties that can help to clear out the airways. These remedies can be used in combination with rest, fluids, and a healthy diet to help our bodies recover from a cold.
supplementary_related_ailments: When you have a cold, your body fights off the virus, but it can also feel miserable. People with colds often experience headaches due to inflammation and congestion in the sinuses. Echinacea and ginger can help reduce inflammation and ease pain. Echinacea boosts the immune system, while ginger's anti-inflammatory properties ease headache pain.  Coughing is another common symptom of colds. Thyme and licorice root can help soothe the throat and reduce coughing. Thyme contains compounds that kill bacteria and viruses, while licorice root's expectorant properties help loosen mucus.  Colds can also cause fatigue and a sore throat. Slippery elm and marshmallow root can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing discomfort. Their anti-inflammatory properties also help ease pain.  Sinus congestion and pressure often accompany colds. Eucalyptus and peppermint can help relieve congestion and ease sinus pressure. Eucalyptus's decongestant properties help open airways, while peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties ease sinus pain.  Fever and chills are also common symptoms of colds. Yarrow and elderberry can help reduce fever and ease chills. Yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation, while elderberry's antiviral properties help fight off the virus.  These medicinal herbs can be used in teas, tinctures, or supplements to help alleviate cold symptoms.
